action.reasoning: Observation: Residual UNet val_loss=0.0227 at epoch 4, then plateaus. Best eval so far is rmse_z500=479 from iter 2. All models overfit severely. Analysis: The val_loss plateau at ~0.022-0.023 seems to be a fundamental limit for this architecture. Need to try a different approach. Key insight: Vision Transformers with global attention might capture large-scale weather patterns better than local CNNs. Also, I should try: (1) ViT with patch embedding, (2) more aggressive augmentation including latitude flipping, (3) ensemble with persistence. Let me implement a ViT-based model. Also, the current best checkpoint (iter 2, rmse_z500=479) should be submitted. Decision: Implement ViT model with patch size 4x4 on 32x64 grid = 128 patches, with global attention. Also try stronger augmentation.
action.command: MODEL_TYPE=hybrid BASE_CH=64 EMBED_DIM=256 DEPTH=6 DROPOUT=0.1 LR=3e-4 WEIGHT_DECAY=1e-4 NUM_EPOCHS=50 RESIDUAL=1 python train.py
